system.description:
When you reach 6th level, your reputation as a fire lord grows, shaping how people around you perceive you. You add half your proficiency bonus to your Prestige rating.

In addition, your reputation strongly affects how you are perceived, and when it becomes known that you are in a settlement people treat you accordingly. At the Narrator’s discretion, there may be settlements (such as an enemy’s war camp) where you cannot utilize this feature. Choose one of the following:

Pyral Icon

When you arrive in a settlement and reveal who you are, the faithful and the desperate flock to you. Local leaders offer shelter, temples burn incense in your honor, and those in need beg for your guidance or miracles.

  • You gain an expertise die on Religion or Persuasion checks (your choice when you gain this feature).

  • You may ask a local temple or shrine for a boon (lodging, food, minor magical aid), and they will comply unless doing so would endanger the community.

Ashwalker

You are known as a destroyer—ruthless, relentless, a walking omen. When you enter a settlement, commoners clear the streets, shopkeepers shutter their doors, and rumor spreads like wildfire. People obey you out of fear, not loyalty.

  • You gain an expertise die on Intimidation or Insight checks (your choice when you gain this feature).

  • Once per week, you can compel someone to answer a question truthfully, as if affected by @UUID[Compendium.a5e.a5e-spells.Item.i0o4xqqd86vdkwwu]{Zone of Truth}, unless they succeed a Wisdom saving throw against your Intimidation check.

Flamebound Freeblade

Your name is whispered among mercenaries, criminals, and traveling mages as one who gets things done—sometimes too well. The city guard keeps watch on you, but underworld fixers or arcanists may offer jobs or favors.

  • You gain an expertise die on Deception or Sleight of Hand (your choice when you gain this feature).

  • You can ask local fences, smugglers, or mercenaries for minor illegal services or information. They treat you as a high-value client.
